intro: JavaScript's flexible execution engine works well for broad web scripting but lacks strict runtime guarantees. Moving to Swift transitions applications to a compiled, type-safe ecosystem engineered for performance on Apple platforms and optimized backend environments. This shift helps development teams eliminate runtime errors, use advanced value types, and maximize hardware utilization.

diffs.to: Static, strong compile-time validation with type inference.
Automatic Reference Counting (ARC) tracking instances at compile time.

diffs.from: Variables can be null or undefined, risking runtime errors.
diffs.to: Compile-time Optional types that prevent unexpected null access.

diffs.from: JIT-compiled bytecode executed inside a dedicated engine loop.
diffs.to: Ahead-of-time compiled directly into native machine code.

diffs.from: Dynamic object structures and flexible array sequences.
diffs.to: Explicit value types (structs, enums) and reference types (classes).

examples.title: Safe Value Unwrapping
examples.description: Accessing optional fields securely without causing app crashes.

examples.title: Value Type Structs
examples.description: Defining lightweight, thread-safe models using structural configurations.

tips.title: Break Reference Cycles
tips.body: Mark closure references and delegates as weak or unowned to prevent memory leaks under ARC.

tips.title: Prefer Structs by Default
tips.body: Use structs for data models instead of classes to take advantage of value semantics and thread safety.

tips.title: Unwrap Optionals Safely
tips.body: Use if-let or guard-let patterns to unwrap optional values cleanly without risking runtime crashes.

tips.title: Declare Constants with let
tips.body: Enforce data immutability by using "let" for all stable values and "var" only when values must change.
